ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general iced tea class; no specific black tea lemonade entry exists. FOODON_03301099 used as closest parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 AN0KN covers ready-to-drink tea broadly; no specific black tea lemonade with citrus code exists. Used as closest defensible parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34111008 covers tea as a substance; no specific iced black tea lemonade concept exists. Used as closest parent.

preparation_method: blended with lemon juice or lemonade

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard tall serving of Iced Shaken Black Tea Lemonade is approximately 355 g (12 fl oz), as typically served at Starbucks Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Starbucks Iced Black Tea Lemonade — Nutrition Information (Tall, no classic syrup variant cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central: Lemonade and Brewed Black Tea components)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods) — component-based estimation; cross-referenced with Starbucks published nutrition data
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Starbucks Singapore does not publish per-100g nutrient data. Values estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data: brewed black tea (USDA FDC #171917) and lemonade (USDA FDC #167762), blended in approximate 50:50 ratio by volume as typical for this product. Energy and macros reflect the dilute, low-calorie nature of this beverage. Sugar content reflects lemonade contribution. Sodium is low. Values are per 100g of the final beverage.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of lemonade and tea-based beverages — analogous estimate based on lemonade GI data from University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Starbucks Iced Shaken Black Tea Lemonade. GI estimated at 40 (low category) based on: (1) plain brewed black tea has negligible GI due to near-zero carbohydrate content; (2) lemonade with sugar has a GI of approximately 54 per University of Sydney GI Database (lemonade, commercially prepared); (3) the 50:50 blend dilutes the lemonade contribution, yielding an estimated blended GI of approximately 40. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (40 × 15.975) / 100 = 6.39, rounded to 6.4. Carbohydrate per serving is 4.5g/100g * 355g = 15.975g.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (approximately 4 mg per 100g; ~14 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Iced Shaken Black Tea Lemonade is very low in sodium and poses minimal concern for individuals managing hypertension, though patients should be aware that added syrups or customisations at point of sale can significantly increase sodium and sugar content.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Free sugar content from lemonade component (approximately 4.2g sugar per 100g; ~15g per tall serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume Iced Shaken Black Tea Lemonade with caution due to its free sugar content from the lemonade component; requesting the unsweetened or reduced-syrup version and monitoring portion size is recommended to limit postprandial glucose excursion.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat and cholesterol; low sodium; black tea polyphenols may confer modest cardioprotective benefit
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Iced Shaken Black Tea Lemonade is very low in saturated fat, cholesterol, and sodium, making it a generally acceptable beverage for individuals with cardiovascular disease, though added sugars from the lemonade component should be factored into overall daily free sugar intake targets.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Iced Shaken Black Tea Lemonade is a cold beverage made by brewing full-bodied black tea, combining it with lemonade (a mixture of lemon juice, water, and sugar), and shaking the blend vigorously with ice cubes to create a chilled, lightly frothy drink. The shaking process aerates the beverage and produces a refreshing, slightly tart flavour profile with the astringency of black tea balanced by the citrus brightness of lemon and the sweetness of the lemonade base. In Singapore, this drink is sold at Starbucks outlets and is categorised under the café's iced tea range. Nutritionally, it is a low-calorie beverage at approximately 18 kcal per 100g, with minimal protein and fat. Its primary macronutrient contribution is carbohydrate from the lemonade sugar content (approximately 4.5g per 100g). The drink contains a small amount of vitamin C from the lemon component and trace minerals from the tea. Black tea contributes polyphenols including theaflavins and thearubigins, which have been associated with modest antioxidant and cardioprotective effects in epidemiological research. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 40 (low category), reflecting the dilution of the lemonade sugar by the tea component. For individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, the free sugar content warrants portion awareness. For those managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease, the very low sodium and fat content make this a generally suitable beverage choice when consumed without additional syrups or sweeteners.

confidence_reason: No exact per-100g nutrient data is published by Starbucks Singapore for this specific product. Values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for brewed black tea and lemonade in approximate proportions. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database lemonade data. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are approved primary databases and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: Fat_g is confirmed zero by formulation — this is a tea and lemonade beverage containing no fat-bearing ingredients. Saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and cholesterol_mg are all confirmed zero by product formulation. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as this is a liquid beverage with no fibre-bearing solids. Vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA component sources for this beverage type. Vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. Vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. Vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. Vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. Vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. Folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. Vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. Vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. Phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. Magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. Zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. Selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. Glycemic_index set to 40 (low category) as an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement exists for this product. Serving size of 355g corresponds to a Starbucks tall (12 fl oz) cup. This record is sourced from field survey data (GD001113) and served at Starbucks One Fullerton, Singapore. Price SGD 7.20. The multilingual naming follows Singaporean café beverage conventions; this is a Western-origin café product sold in Singapore, so Singaporean multilingual rules apply. Hindi alias correction: the Devanagari string 'आइस्ड शेकन ब्लैक टी लेमनेड' uses standard Devanagari script throughout; verified character consistency between aliases_by_language and flat aliases list.
